desc:
This once was a place filled with people. Now, only a withered well standing in
the middle of the square is left from those days. Looking west, you see the
large gate to the citadel, with its towers stretching into the sky. A road
leaves the square to the east, taking you downhill.
name: The Square at the Castle

desc:
Crumbling stone walls are the only remnants of a large house - once the home of
the hundred soldiers that once was the Royal Guard of Arthedain. The roof has
fallen in, and you see no traces of any living beings. You shiver as you look
around and see the piles of human bones that reminds of the horrible slaughter
that once took place within these walls.
name: The Old Barracks

desc:
A small room lies here, just behind the gate of the castles' main building. You
can go back outside to the east or up a wide stone stair to the west. Stretching
over twenty yards from the north to the south, this is truly a hallway worthy of
a king.
name: Behind the Gate

desc:
You have entered the impressive halls of the old kings. Once, great feasts were
held here, but now, there is nothing that seems joyous about it. Where once
great chandeliers lit the halls, tables now lie broken on the floor. The hall
continues to the north and the west. A wide staircase leads down to the east, to
the gate of the citadel.
name: The Great Hall

desc:
This is the north east part of the large hall. There are piles of dust and old
cobwebs on the floor, and you feel very small as you walk through the dark halls
of the Arthedain kings.
name: The Great Hall

desc:
The south west corner of the hall lies in complete darkness, but you can make
out some kind of tunnel or passage leading south of here. It certainly doesn't
look very inviting. A broken stone table lies on the floor, covered with ugly
marks made by foul orcs long ago.
name: The Great Hall

desc:
Darkness surrounds you as you try to make your way south through the passage.
Your flickering light is not strong enough to give any signs of what awaits you
in the pitch black tunnel.
name: The Dark Passage

desc:
This room really gives you the creeps. The dungeons hidden way down in the
ground below the citadel above haven't been used by living beings in nearly a
thousand years. Rusted chains are fastened to the walls and in them, skeletons
of tortured men still hang - the result from the cruelty of the horrid orcs that
overtook Fornost in the year of 1974.
name: In a Prison Cell

desc:
You have now entered what seems to be the dungeons of the old castle. There are
remains of since long dead prisoners, left to die in here by the foul orcs in a
forgotten past. Metal doors are to the south and the north, and to the east the
corridor leads into a small room.
name: The Dungeons

desc:
Once, the prison keeper lived in this square chamber. Strange and abhorrent
torturing tools lie rusting and withering on a bench. In one corner you see
charred bits of a wooden bed.
name: The Prison Keeper's Chamber

desc:
A small stone stair reaches down from the dark tunnel above and ends in this
moist cellar. The air is very heavy to breathe, and you have much trouble trying
to find your way through the small passages.
name: In the Damp Cellar

desc:
This old cellar was obviously razed in the raging battles that once took place
in the castle. The north and west walls lie in a pile of rubble, the south wall
still stands, and you can go back to the rest of the cellar to the east.
name: In the Damp Cellar

desc:
The air is getting heavier and heavier to breathe. It is very moist, and you
feel your clothes clinging to your body as you carefully move around in the old
cellar. In the flickering light of your lantern, you see no other way out, but
back to the east. A sudden gust of air makes you aware of a terrible stench
coming from somewhere in the darkness that surrounds you.
name: In the Damp Cellar

desc:
Dark rock walls surround you, as you walk around in the cellar. The rooms are
cut directly out of the high hill that the castle stands upon. However, from the
perfectly smooth walls, you can easily tell that these cellars were made by
skilled Dunedain.
name: In the Damp Cellar

desc:
The stench in here is barely bearable. You find yourself standing on the
beginning of some kind of marble quay that leads out in a pool with black, oily
water.
name: By the Stinking Pool

desc:
This is the end of the quay. To the east, the underground pool stretches into
the darkness. Watching the water carefully, you see that small ripples keep
coming in against the stones. Silently, you wonder what might be out there, in
the black water of the pool.
name: By the Stinking Pool

desc:
Slowly you paddle your way through the black water. The oily surface lies
silent, except for slow ripples that keep coming from the centre of the pool
somewhere out there. You are amazed at how very how large this cave is and
indeed, large is its inhabitant too...
name: In the Stinking Pool

desc:
The water quickly rushes down the sloping tunnel, carrying you away from the
horrible kraken that lies dead above. What is that? Suddenly you catch a glimpse
of something standing on a small ledge next to the stream that carries you
forward. If you hurry, you might have time to grab it...
name: In the Quick Stream of Water

desc:
Terror creeps upon you as you look around in the chamber of death and violence.
Here, many prisoners suffered a fate worse than death itself, under the
supervision of the skilled orkish torturers. This room is no place for someone
of the living world...
name: In a Prison Cell

desc:
Looking up, you see that the staircase ends here, and leads out in some kind of
room above you. You cannot see anything of what the room contains, but a thick
layer of dust tells you that no one has walked here in many, many years.
name: The End of the Winding Staircase

desc:
The wide staircase leads you up, into a tower in the north-west side of the
castle. Looking back down, you see the beginning of the great hall, but you can
see nothing of what lies ahead of you as you gaze into the darkness of the
stairs to the south.
name: The Winding Staircase

desc:
Still climbing upwards, progress is very hard. The staircase takes you in a
spiral up the circular tower. You can go either down, to the north, or up to the
east.
name: The Winding Staircase

desc:
The stairs slowly circle their way upwards in the tower to the north and down to
the west. A small window in the thick stone wall lets some fresh air in here,
and you see that the layer of dust is somewhat thinner ...
name: The Winding Staircase

desc:
The balcony you are standing on is large and robust. It is fifty feet wide and
reaches some ten feet from the tower wall behind you. Turning your gaze to the
south, you see a forest covered area, with the Iaur Men Formen, the Old North
Road, reaching south, towards Bree.
name: On the Balcony

desc:
You push disgusting thick cobwebs to the side and try to make your way forward
without falling on any hidden objects (or treasures?) on the floor. A rusty iron
door leads out onto an old balcony to the south. The dark hall continues to the
north and the west.
name: The Large Hall in the Tower

desc:
You have reached the north-eastern corner of the large hall. A winding staircase
can take you back down to the more inviting safety below you. The wind howls
around the tower making you feel slightly frightened of entering this large
cobweb filled room.
name: The Large Hall in the Tower

desc:
This large hall is situated about half-way up the unusually well preserved
northwestern tower of the citadel. Large cobwebs hang across the walls, and in
each corner of this long since abandoned room, and an occasional strange
creaking noise can be heard, as if something were crossing the uneven wooden
floor behind you.
name: The Large Hall in the Tower

desc:
Cobwebs cling to everything in this room, and you feel yourself entangled by
their soft silk every time you move. The sticky strands seem to be stronger than
any normal spider's web however. Faint scuffling and an occasional louder
clattering can be heard from somewhere in the darkness nearby.
name: The Large Hall in the Tower

desc:
Ah! You have found a secret staircase behind the curtain. You carefully peer up
the stairs, but the darkness above is impenetrable by your weak eyes. A very
thick layer of dust lies on the ground, with no traces of any being of flesh and
blood having walked here in hundreds of years.
name: The Secret Staircase

desc:
A breathtaking view lies below you, as you look over the walls that prevent you
from falling down on the castle yard several hundred feet below. Looking around,
you see all of Arthedain stretching into the horizon. Below you, the city of
Fornost lies quietly, with the ruined western part and the rebuilt eastern part.
name: The Top of the Tower

desc:
You have reached the last room in the tower. In the middle of the room stands a
sturdy stone pillar, with a perfect half sphere cut out of it. Recalling the old
tales of the wise men at home, you realise that this must be the site of the
Palantir that once was kept in Fornost Erain to communicate with the southern
kingdom, Gondor. However, Arvedui, the last king of Arthedain, took it with him
into the cold depths of the icy waters of Forochel.
name: The Hidden Room

desc:
An enormous hall stretches to the south and the east. This was once the great
banquet hall of the kings of Arthedain, told to have been one of the finest
places ever built by men in Eriador. There is not much to remind you of its
greatness left. The hall lies shrouded in darkness, and the thick air makes it
heavy to breathe. West of here, you see a small, winding staircase making its
way up into some kind of tower.
name: The Great Hall

desc:
You are now in a small room very near the top of the tower. You shiver as a
howling sound breaks the silence, and you desperately try to convince yourself
that it was nothing but the wind trying to tear down the old castle. It doesn't
look as if there was a battle fought in this room, since it, as opposed to the
rest of the castle, doesn't have bones and rusted weapons lying all over the
floor.
name: The Tower Room
